chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Dislike of Traveling Alone

text: 2434 - وَ رَوَى إِبْرَاهِيمُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَلْحَمِيدِ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لَعَنَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ثَلاَثَةً اَلْآكِلَ زَادَهُ وَحْدَهُ وَ اَلنَّائِمَ فِي بَيْتٍ وَحْدَهُ وَ اَلرَّاكِبَ فِي اَلْفَلاَةِ وَحْدَهُ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2434 - Ibrahim ibn Abdul-Hamid narrated from Abu al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), who said:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings of Allah (swt) be upon him and his family) cursed three: the one who eats their provisions alone, the one who sleeps alone in a house, and the one who travels alone in the wilderness."
